project1_desc: Solves the problem of overly long AI follow-up questions that degrade respondent experience. Dynamically controls question length based on answer length, device type, and interview mode.
project3_desc: Automatically translates vague client requirements into executable technical solutions. Supports conflict detection, fuzzy marking, and one-click TRD generation.
home_subtitle: Two core optimization projects for the Quria AI Interview System
answer_placeholder: Enter a simulated respondent answer (e.g., I think the product is okay, but the price is a bit high)
requirement_placeholder: Enter client requirement (e.g., We want to deeply understand young mothers' purchase decisions, the more samples the better, deliver ASAP)
chat_placeholder: Type the respondent's answer here — the AI interviewer will follow up turn by turn…
generate_question_hint: Click "Generate follow-up" to see it in action
docs_card_desc: Complete PRD and SRS documents, including user stories, acceptance criteria, and technical architecture.
docs_error: Failed to load the document. Please retry or view it on GitHub.
home_footer: Designed from hands-on Quria product experience · 2026
error_desc: An unexpected error occurred while rendering. You can retry or go back home.
p3_welcome: 👋 Hi! I'm the requirement decomposition assistant. Send me a vague client requirement and I'll break it down into a structured plan, run a quality check, and even generate a technical requirements document (TRD) in one click.
p3_decompose_done: Done! Research goal: {goal}; suggested sample {sample}; est. duration {duration}. See the full plan in the card below:
p3_check_ok: Quality check passed: clear requirements, no obvious conflicts.
p3_check_found: Quality check complete. Found {fuzzy} fuzzy terms and {conflict} potential conflicts — details below:
p3_trd_intro: TRD generated. Preview below (export Markdown / PDF, or copy the full text):
p3_reset_done: Reset. Send me a new requirement.
rhythm_suggestion: ⚠️ The respondent seems resistant — the AI has dropped the current topic and switched to an easy question to rebuild rapport.
rhythm_chart_label: Answer length per turn (higher = more engaged)
